English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Hermann Herrey papers
Herrey, Hermann, 1904-1968.
3476
16.5 cubic feet. 16.5 cubic feet.
[ca.1939]-1971. Drawings, paintings, plans and photographs (including glass plate negatives), planning projects, stage designs, industrial designs, furniture, inventions, correspondence, biographical material, lecture notes, talks, reports, and manuscripts. Drawings, paintings, plans and photographs (including glass plate negatives) of architectural work, planning projects, stage designs, industrial designs, furniture and inventions. Also includes correspondence, biographical material, lecture notes, talks and reports; writings in city and regional planning, economic development, architecture, theater and related fields. Also, manuscript of a novel written by Herrey in the 1930s; and a play written under the pseudonym of Gilbert Tanner, ca.1960. Collection material in English, German, and French <<<<<<< HEAD b 9 4 5 e k j 8 7 6 l f 3 1 1 2 ? (unprocessed) ? (unprocessed) ? (unprocessed) ? (unprocessed) ? (unprocessed) ======= b 9 4 5 e k j 8 7 6 l f 3 1 1 2 ? (unprocessed) ? (unprocessed) ? (unprocessed) ? (unprocessed) ? (unprocessed) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2068825
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
B. Shepherd, B. Huth
Date completed:
July 1981
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, May 2002 Julia Guarneri, June 2002
Date modified:
Ben Wrubel, October 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Drawings, paintings, plans and photographs (including glass plate negatives) of architectural work, planning projects, stage designs, industrial designs, furniture and inventions. Also includes correspondence, biographical material, lecture notes, talks and reports; writings in city and regional planning, economic development, architecture, theater and related fields. Also, manuscript of a novel written by Herrey in the 1930s; and a play written under the pseudonym of Gilbert Tanner, ca.1960.
Cite As:
Hermann Herrey Papers, #3476. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Director of classical and modern drama, architect, city planner. A native of Vienna, Hermann Herrey was educated in Vienna and Berlin, and worked as a stage designer in Germany and England before coming to the United States in 1940, at the invitation of Walter Gropius. Herrey adapted and rewrote modern and classical plays and translated other plays into English and German; designed stage sets and costumes; and lectured at colleges and universities, including Harvard University, McGill University, Yale University, Queens College and The American Academy of Arts and Sciences.
Restrictions on Use:
Copyright retained by donor.
Restrictions on Use:
Permission to cite or quote from the collection must be obtained in writing from the donor's representative.
Paintings. Lecture notes. Drawings. Theatrical producers and directors. City planners. Architects. Theaters -- Stage-setting and scenery. Stage adaptions. Regional planning. City planning. Theater. Playwriting. Industrial design. Furniture design. Drama -- 20th century. Costume design. Classical drama. Architectural design. Tanner, Gilbert.

Scope and Contents
Photographs from plays directed and designed by Hermann Herrey. Contains: "Jacques ou la Soumission", "Les Bonnes", "The Chairs", "Hedda Gabler", "Landschaft mit Figuren","Konig Nicolo", "Julius Caesar", "Macbeth", "The Taming of the Shrew""Hauptstadt Berlin: Ergebnis des internationalen stadtebaulichen Ideenwettbewerbs"196010Blueprints of a Parking Garage (?)10Architectural interestMisc. correspondence, c.1944Not 10Letters, etc,. of introduction, by H.H. and for H.H.,1938-1951Not 10Queen's College correspondence, c.1944Not 10Correspondence re. projects, c.1954-55Not 10Metropolitan Planning - Correspondence1945-1951825Correspondence - Letters of Introduction by and for Hermann Herrey1938-1951826Correspondence War Manpower Commission 1943827Correspondence Misc 1953828Articles- Newspapers and MagazinesHermann Herrey papers,1939-19719Photo Album1954-196010
Scope and Contents
Photographs from plays directed and designed by Hermann Herrey. Contains: "Jacques ou la Soumission", "Les Bonnes", "The Chairs", "Hedda Gabler", "Landschaft mit Figuren","Konig Nicolo", "Julius Caesar", "Macbeth", "The Taming of the Shrew""Hauptstadt Berlin: Ergebnis des internationalen stadtebaulichen Ideenwettbewerbs"196010Blueprints of a Parking Garage (?)10Architectural interestMisc. correspondence, c.1944Not 10Letters, etc,. of introduction, by H.H. and for H.H.,1938-1951Not 10Queen's College correspondence, c.1944Not 10Correspondence re. projects, c.1954-55Not 10Metropolitan Planning - ManhattanNot 10Misc. plansNot 10NYT MagazineNot 10NYTNot 10Daily NewsNot 101Berliner TageblattNot 102SudkurierNot 103New York PostNot 104The Architectural ReviewNot 101Die DameNot 102The Architectural ReviewNot 103Illustrirte ZeitungNot 104Theatre interestFolder relating to production of "King Nicolo" or "Such is Life" by Frank Wedekind (correspondence and script)11Correspondence publicity re. the above play11Drama Exchange Plan,1956-195711Folder of correspondence concerning London theatres, also "On the Run" by Robert Vansittart, 1939-1940 (production deferred)1939-194011Note: "King Nicolo" as adapted, produced, and directed by H.H.11Many notes on the theatre11Diploma of German Critics Award to H.H.,1958-195911"N" or "This is Our World" by Gilbert Tanner -
